Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: this is a detailed process mining task The user has provided an event log with four cases and wants me to identify anomalies in the Procure to Pay process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me start by recalling the standard P2P sequence it should follow requisition approval purchase order goods receipt invoice payment closure

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at Case 1001 the sequence seems correct and follows the ideal flow No issues there

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 immediately stands out because the purchase order was issued before the requisition was approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thats a clear violation of controls

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Also payment was released before goods receipt and even before approval

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 has payment released before goods receipt and invoice matching

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thats problematic because payment should only happen after verifying goods and matching invoices

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 shows goods received before the purchase order was issued

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: which breaks the fundamental principle that a PO must exist before procurement

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: I should explain each anomaly clearly linking them to business risks like fraud financial loss or compliance issues

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: The user likely wants to understand not just whats wrong but why it matters in practice Ill structure the response by case highlighting the deviations and their implications
